code: wl_error = function(rush) {    repeat {     x1 = runif(1)     x2 = runif(1)      xss = list(list(x1 = x1, x2 = x2))      key = rush$push_running_tasks(xss = xss)      if (x1 > 0.90) {       stop("Unexpected error")     }      rush$finish_tasks(key, yss = list(list(y = x1 + x2)))   } } rush_worker = RushWorker$new(network_id = "test-error")  wl_error(rush_worker) Error in `wl_error()`: ! Unexpected error rush = rsh(   network = "test-error",   config = redux::redis_config())  mirai::daemons(1)  rush$start_workers(   worker_loop = wl_error,   n_workers = 1) rush$detect_lost_workers() [1] "describable_whiterhino" rush = rsh(   network = "test-error",   config = redux::redis_config())  message_log = tempdir() output_log = tempdir()  mirai::daemons(1)  worker_ids = rush$start_workers(   worker_loop = wl_error,   n_workers = 1,   message_log = message_log,   output_log = output_log)  Sys.sleep(5)  readLines(file.path(message_log, sprintf("message_%s.log", worker_ids[1]))) [1] "Debug message logging on worker effaceable_iberianemeraldlizard started" readLines(file.path(output_log, sprintf("output_%s.log", worker_ids[1]))) [1] "[1] \"Debug output logging on worker effaceable_iberianemeraldlizard started\""

title: Rush Manager
text: mirai package provides mechanism launching rush workers local remote machines. mirai daemons persistent background processes execute arbitrary R code parallel. Daemons started using mirai::daemons(). local daemons, number workers specified. daemons started, workers launched $start_workers() method. $wait_for_workers() method blocks workers registered network. Worker information accessible $worker_info field. worker identified worker_id. pid field denotes process identifier hostname field indicates machine name. remote column specifies whether worker remote heartbeat column indicates presence heartbeat process. state column reflects current worker state, can "running" "terminated". Workers can stopped individually . terminate specific worker, $stop_workers() method called corresponding worker_ids. stop workers reset network, $reset() method used. Instead killing worker processes, manager can send terminate signal. worker terminates completing current task. worker loop must check rush$terminated flag. $stop_workers() method type = "terminate" sends terminate signal. Failed workers started mirai automatically detected manager. simulate worker crash killing worker process. Daemons can also launched remote machines via SSH. high-performance computing clusters, daemons can started using scheduler. rush integrated third-party package, worker startup typically managed package . Users can configure worker options calling rush_plan() function, specifies number workers, worker type, Redis configuration. Arguments required worker loop passed named arguments $start_workers(). arguments serialized stored Redis database part worker configuration. Upon initialization, worker retrieves deserializes configuration executing worker loop. Note maximum size Redis string 512 MiB. serialized worker configuration exceeds limit, rush raises error. manager workers share access file system, rush instead write large objects disk. large_objects_path argument rush_plan() specifies directory used storing objects. Workers can record messages generated via lgr package database. lgr_thresholds argument $start_local_workers() specifies logging level logger, e.g. c("mlr3/rush" = "debug"). Logging introduces minor performance overhead disabled default. recent log messages can retrieved follows. retrieve log entries, use $read_log() method.

title: Rush Manager
text: $worker_script() method generates R script can executed machine access Redis database. flexible mechanism starting workers, imposes constraints execution environment. Workers started processx mirai monitored automatically respective packages. Script workers require explicit heartbeat mechanism detect failures. heartbeat consists Redis key set expiration timeout, refreshed periodically background process linked main worker process. worker fails, heartbeat process also ceases, key expires, manager marks worker "terminated". heartbeat_period heartbeat_expire arguments configure heartbeat startup. heartbeat_period defines refresh interval seconds; heartbeat_expire sets expiration duration, must exceed heartbeat period. kill script worker, $stop_workers(type = "kill") method pushes kill signal heartbeat process, terminates main worker process.

title: rush - Asynchronous and Distributed Computing
text: rush network consists multiple workers communicate via shared Redis database. worker evaluates tasks pushes corresponding results back database, illustrated Figure 1. Figure 1: communication flow worker Redis database rush network. octagon represents worker rectangle represents Redis database. worker W runs instance optimizer \(\mathcal{O}\), evaluates objective function \(f\), exchanges task information via shared Redis database. Arrows indicate flow information: workers retrieve completed tasks via $fetch_finished_tasks(), propose store new tasks via $push_running_tasks(), report results via $finish_tasks().

title: rush - Asynchronous and Distributed Computing
text: begin simple random search illustrate core concepts rush. Although random search require communication workers, introduces worker loop, tasks, manager. use Branin function \(f\) optimization target: \[f(x_1,x_2)=\left(x_2-\frac{5.1}{4\pi^2}x_1^2+\frac{5}{\pi}x_1-6\right)^2 +10\left(1-\frac{1}{8\pi}\right)\cos(x_1)+10\] function optimized domain \(x_1 \[-5, 10]\) \(x_2 \[0, 15]\). commonly used benchmark function fast evaluate yet sufficiently nontrivial.  Branin function define worker_loop function, executed worker. function repeatedly samples random point, evaluates using Branin function, writes result Redis database. takes RushWorker object (rush) objective function branin arguments. loop terminates 100 tasks evaluated. worker loop relies two principal methods. $push_running_tasks() method creates new task database, marks "running", returns unique key identifying . $finish_tasks() method takes key along result writes database, marking task "finished". $n_finished_tasks field tracks number completed tasks serves termination criterion. Marking task "running" evaluation essential random search, important algorithms use states workers’ tasks inform next proposal. Tasks basic units workers exchange information. task consists four components: unique key, computational state, input (xs), output (ys). input output lists may contain arbitrary data. Tasks pass one four computational states: "running", "finished", "failed", "queued". $push_running_tasks() method creates tasks marked "running" returns keys. Upon completion, $finish_tasks() marks tasks "finished" stores associated results. Tasks encounter errors can marked "failed" using $fail_tasks() method. fourth state, "queued", supports queue mechanism described Section 4.1. Rush manager class responsible starting, monitoring, stopping workers within network. initialized using rsh() function, requires network identifier config argument. config argument specifies configuration file used connect Redis database via redux package. Workers started using $start_workers() method accepts worker loop number workers arguments. additional named arguments forwarded worker loop function. workers run mirai daemons started mirai::daemons() function. optimization completes, results can retrieved database. $fetch_finished_tasks() method returns data.table containing task key, input, result. worker_id column identifies worker evaluated task. auxiliary information can passed $push_running_tasks() $finish_tasks() via extra argument. Printing rush object displays number running workers number tasks state. Note total tasks slightly exceeds 100 workers check stopping condition independently: multiple workers evaluate condition concurrently — example, 99 tasks finished — may create new task detecting limit reached. workers can stopped database reset using $reset() method.

code: wl_median_stopping = function(rush, training_ids, test_ids, mtcars_data, response) {   while (rush$n_finished_tasks < 1000) {     params = list(       max_depth = sample(1:20, 1),       lambda = runif(1, 0, 1),       alpha = runif(1, 0, 1)     )      model = NULL     for (iteration in seq(5, 20)) {        key = rush$push_running_tasks(xss = list(c(params, list(nrounds = iteration))))        model = xgboost::xgboost(         data = as.matrix(mtcars_data[training_ids, ]),         label = response[training_ids],         nrounds = if (is.null(model)) 5 else 1,         params = params,         xgb_model = model,         verbose = 0       )        pred = predict(model, as.matrix(mtcars_data[test_ids, ]))       rmse = sqrt(mean((pred - response[test_ids])^2))        rush$finish_tasks(key, yss = list(list(rmse = rmse)))        tasks = rush$fetch_finished_tasks()       ref = tasks[nrounds == iteration, rmse]       if (length(ref) > 0 && rmse > median(ref)) break     }   } } data(mtcars)  training_ids = sample(1:nrow(mtcars), 20) test_ids = setdiff(1:nrow(mtcars), training_ids) mtcars_data = mtcars[, -1] response = mtcars$mpg  config = redux::redis_config()  rush = rsh(   network = "example-median-stopping",   config = config)  mirai::daemons(4)  rush$start_workers(   worker_loop = wl_median_stopping,   n_workers = 4,   training_ids = training_ids,   test_ids = test_ids,   mtcars_data = mtcars_data,   response = response) rush$fetch_finished_tasks()[order(rmse)] worker_id max_depth    lambda     alpha nrounds     rmse          keys           <char>     <int>     <num>     <num>   <int>    <num>        <char> 1: calx_dassi...         4 0.9225005 0.6509440       5 3.124495 3ce4ac8e-5... 2: godly_berg...         4 0.4612483 0.9443685       5 3.124495 1b90dbb6-b... 3: taxonomica...         7 0.1978189 0.9864322       5 3.124495 23ddfd5a-4... 4: sugar_indi...        14 0.7559320 0.6333063       5 3.124495 51bc94de-2... 5: calx_dassi...         4 0.9225005 0.6509440       6 3.983723 212839c0-9... 6: godly_berg...         4 0.4612483 0.9443685       6 3.983723 620467b8-4... 7: sugar_indi...        14 0.7559320 0.6333063       6 3.983723 a734ab07-3... 8: taxonomica...         7 0.1978189 0.9864322       6 3.983723 8dbe7f06-1... 9: calx_dassi...         4 0.9225005 0.6509440       7 3.983723 7d7d1b21-e... rush$reset()

title: rush - Asynchronous and Distributed Computing
text: implement Asynchronous Decentralized Bayesian Optimization (ADBO) (Egelé et al. 2023) demonstrates use shared task information queue mechanism. ADBO runs sequential Bayesian optimization multiple workers parallel. worker maintains surrogate model independently proposes next configuration maximizing upper confidence bound acquisition function. promote varying exploration–exploitation trade-offs across workers, \(\lambda\) parameter acquisition function sampled independently worker. worker completes evaluation, shares result via database; workers incorporate information local surrogate models next iteration. typical task lifecycle rush running finished, package also supports queue mechanism cases tasks created centrally distributed workers. initialize rush network push initial Latin hypercube sampling (LHS) design queue. Structured designs LHS can outperform random designs, generating requires global view design space. queue avoids redundant evaluations: design generated main process, workers draw tasks shared queue. worker loop first drains initial design queue using $pop_task() method, retrieves next queued task, marks "running", returns . queue empty, $pop_task() returns NULL, signaling transition model-based optimization phase. $fetch_tasks_with_state() method retrieves tasks specified states database, returning data.table containing task states, keys, inputs, results. Using $fetch_tasks_with_state() rather separate calls $fetch_running_tasks() $fetch_finished_tasks() prevents tasks appearing twice state transition occurs fetch. start four workers wait optimization complete.
code: config = redux::redis_config()  rush = rsh(   network = "example-bayesian-optimization",   config = config) lhs_points = lhs::maximinLHS(n = 25, k = 2) x1_lower = -5 x1_range = 15 x2_lower = 0 x2_range = 15  xss = lapply(1:25, function(i) {   # rescale to the domain   list(x1 = lhs_points[i, 1] * x1_range + x1_lower, x2 = lhs_points[i, 2] * x2_range + x2_lower) })  rush$push_tasks(xss = xss)  rush ── <Rush> ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── • Running Workers: 0 • Queued Tasks: 25 • Running Tasks: 0 • Finished Tasks: 0 • Failed Tasks: 0 wl_bayesian_optimization = function(rush, branin) {   repeat {     task = rush$pop_task()     if (is.null(task)) break     ys = list(y = branin(task$xs$x1, task$xs$x2))     rush$finish_tasks(task$key, yss = list(ys))   }    lambda = runif(1, 0.01, 10)    while (rush$n_finished_tasks < 100) {      archive = rush$fetch_tasks_with_state(states = c("running", "finished"))     mean_y = mean(archive$y, na.rm = TRUE)     archive["running", y := mean_y, on = "state"]      surrogate = ranger::ranger(       y ~ x1 + x2,       data = archive,       num.trees = 100L,       keep.inbag = TRUE)      xdt = data.table::data.table(x1 = runif(1000, -5, 10), x2 = runif(1000, 0, 15))     p = predict(surrogate, xdt, type = "se", se.method = "jack")     cb = p$predictions - lambda * p$se     xs = as.list(xdt[which.min(cb)])      key = rush$push_running_tasks(xss = list(xs))     ys = list(y = branin(xs$x1, xs$x2))     rush$finish_tasks(key, yss = list(ys))   } } mirai::daemons(4)  rush$start_workers(   worker_loop = wl_bayesian_optimization,   n_workers = 4,   branin = branin) rush$fetch_finished_tasks()[order(y)] worker_id        x1        x2           y          keys             <char>     <num>     <num>       <num>        <char>   1: deflationa... -3.125495 12.704120   0.6179428 d246c8d5-6...   2: deflationa...  2.917920  2.839135   0.7839533 64ad57a5-3...   3: textbookis...  9.107039  1.629358   1.2274135 cbbcd2b8-8...   4: deflationa...  9.444475  1.550778   1.2850178 10c6fe8a-9...   5: insecticid... -2.896562 12.558775   1.4327318 71b46da6-7...  ---  99: colossal_a...  8.584749 13.765313 145.3853483 c0b58d0b-f... 100: insecticid...  8.477147 13.805815 148.7360027 7f34cb87-5... 101: textbookis...  8.775702 14.258832 153.0732176 bc771576-e... 102: colossal_a...  8.686903 14.636062 164.5193098 38d391da-0... 103: colossal_a...  8.103363 14.596816 176.9132472 ccadee7e-b...

title: Rapid Asynchronous and Distributed Computing
text: Package website: release | dev rush package designed solve large-scale problems asynchronously across distributed network. Employing database centric model, rush enables workers communicate tasks results shared Redis database. Key features include low task overhead, efficient caching, robust error handling. package powers asynchronous optimization algorithms bbotk mlr3tuning packages.

title: Rapid Asynchronous and Distributed Computing
text: Database centric model robust scalability. Efficient communication workers usingRedis. Maintains low overhead, limiting delays just millisecond per task. Reduces read/write operations lightweight efficient caching system. Offers centralized system features, task queues. Provides fast data transformation Redis data.table. Simplifies local worker setup processx. Enables scaling large remote worker networks via mirai package. Automatically detects recovers worker failures high reliability. Logs worker messages directly Redis database using lgr. Designed minimal dependencies lightweight integration.

title: Rush — Rush
text: Rush class manages rush network starting, monitoring, stopping workers. shares task-related methods (e.g., fetching results, pushing tasks) RushWorker. Rush instance created rsh() function requires network ID config argument connect Redis database via redux package.

title: Rush — Rush

title: Rush — Rush
text: Tasks unit workers exchange information. main components task key, computational state, input (xs), output (ys). key unique identifier task Redis database. four possible computational states "running", "finished", "failed", "queued". input xs output ys lists can contain arbitrary data. Methods create task: $push_running_tasks(xss): Create running tasks $push_finished_tasks(xss, yss): Create finished tasks. $push_failed_tasks(xss, conditions): Create failed tasks. $push_tasks(xss): Create queued tasks. methods return key created tasks. methods work multiple tasks , xss yss lists inputs outputs. Methods change state existing task: $finish_tasks(keys, yss): Save output tasks mark finished. $fail_tasks(keys, conditions): Mark tasks failed optionally save condition objects. $pop_task(): Pop task queue mark running. following methods used fetch tasks: $fetch_tasks(): Fetch tasks. $fetch_finished_tasks(): Fetch finished tasks. $fetch_failed_tasks(): Fetch failed tasks. $fetch_tasks_with_state(): Fetch tasks different states . $fetch_new_tasks(): Fetch new tasks optionally block new tasks available. methods return data.table() tasks. Tasks following fields: xs: input task. ys: output task. xs_extra: Metadata created creating task. ys_extra: Metadata created finishing task. condition: Condition object task failed. worker_id: id worker created task.

what: rush: Rapid Asynchronous and Distributed Computing — rush-package
title: rush: Rapid Asynchronous and Distributed Computing — rush-package
text: Package tackle large-scale problems asynchronously across distributed network. Employing database centric model, rush enables workers communicate tasks results shared 'Redis' database. Key features include low task overhead, efficient caching, robust error handling. package powers asynchronous optimization algorithms 'bbotk' 'mlr3tuning' packages.

title: rush (development version)
text: feat: Add $push_finished_tasks() method. feat: Combine $wait_for_new_tasks() $fetch_new_tasks() $fetch_new_tasks() timeout argument. refactor: $wait_for_finished_tasks() method removed. perf: Cache now data.table() instead list. data_format argument removed. refactor: Seed mechanism removed. Includes with_rng_state(), is_lecyer_cmrg_seed(), get_random_seed(), set_random_seed(), make_rng_seeds() helper functions. refactor: PID longer stored tasks $push_tasks() $push_running_tasks() methods. refactor: worker_extra field renamed worker_id. refactor: $push_results() renamed $finish_tasks(). fix: Heartbeat process moves worker terminated. refactor: $push_results() deprecated. Use $finish_tasks() instead. refactor: $push_failed() deprecated. Use $fail_tasks() instead. refactor: $terminated_on_idle removed. refactor: Removed $push_priority_tasks(), $n_queued_priority_tasks, $priority_info, $fetch_priority_tasks(). fix: Redis password supported now. refactor: Remove $restart_workers() method. refactor: Squashed "terminated", "killed" "lost" worker states "terminated". feat: Added push_failed_tasks() creating failed tasks. refactor: Remove reset_cache arguments favor $reset_cache() method. refactor: network_id, config connector now active bindings validation. refactor: Removed $snapshot_schedule() $redis_info() methods. refactor: Removed $all_workers_terminated all_workers_lost active bindings. refactor: Moved $worker_states $worker_info. feat: Add "rush.max_object_size" option limit size objects stored Redis. refactor: Renamed $start_remote_workers() $start_workers().
